prompt: I need a new scheduled job that runs every day at 7:00 AM to trigger a daily sales report. Name it "Daily Sales Report", point it to the action URL "https://internal.example.com/reports/sales/trigger", and make sure it's enabled from the start. Tag it with "reports" and "daily".
reference_solution: Confirm the job was created successfully by reviewing the API response.
Report back to the user that the "Daily Sales Report" job has been created, runs every day at 7:00 AM, and is currently enabled.

prompt: Some of our scheduled jobs have been failing recently and need to be temporarily disabled until the engineering team can investigate. Please find all jobs that currently have a "failed" status and disable each one of them.
reference_solution: Call list_jobs with status filter set to "failed" to retrieve all jobs currently in a failed state.

reference_solution.Identify the three failing jobs from the: job-002 (Nightly Log Rotation), job-004 (Hourly Cache Purge), and job-005 (Daily Email Digest).

reference_solution: Call update_job for job-002 with enabled set to false to disable it.
Call update_job for job-004 with enabled set to false to disable it.
Call update_job for job-005 with enabled set to false to disable it.
Confirm to the user that all three failing jobs have been disabled and list their names.

prompt: Our weekly database backup job is currently running on Saturdays, but we need it to run on Sundays at 2:00 AM instead. Please find the backup job and update its cron expression accordingly. Make sure the job remains enabled after the update.
reference_solution: Call list_jobs to retrieve all scheduled jobs and identify the Weekly Database Backup job
Note that job-002 (Weekly Database Backup) has cron_expression "0 2 * * 6" which runs on Saturday (day 6)
Optionally call get_job with job_id job-002 to confirm job details
Call update_job with job_id "job-002", cron_expression "0 2 * * 0" (Sunday is day 0), and enabled true
Confirm the update was successful and report back that the backup job will now run on Sundays at 2:00 AM
task_name: Update Cron Expression for Weekly Backup Job

prompt: We have a job called "Database Health Check" that runs in production, and we need to create a new version of it for our staging environment. The new job should be named "Database Health Check (Staging)", run every 15 minutes instead of the current schedule, be tagged with "staging" and "health-check", and start in a disabled state so we can verify the setup before enabling it. Please set it up based on the existing job's action configuration.

reference_solution.description: Call list_jobs or get_job with job-001 to retrieve the full details of the "Database Health Check" production job, including its action configuration.
Inspect the retrieved job to note the action type (http), URL (https://prod.internal/api/health/db), method (GET), and timeout (30).

Call create_job with the following parameters:
  name: "Database Health Check (Staging)"
  cron_expression: "*/15 * * * *"
  action: {type: "http", url: "https://prod.internal/api/health/db", method: "GET", timeout: 30}
  enabled: false
  tags: ["staging", "health-check"]

Confirm the new job was created successfully by checking the response for the new job ID, and report back to the user that the staging job has been created in a disabled state with a 15-minute schedule.

task_name: Clone and Modify Existing Job for New Environment

prompt: Our scheduler has accumulated several old jobs that are both disabled and tagged as "legacy". Please remove all of those legacy disabled jobs from the system, then provide a summary of the jobs that remain, including their names and current statuses.

reference_solution.description: Call list_jobs with tag filter "legacy" and enabled=false to retrieve all legacy disabled jobs.
Confirm the results include job-002 (Legacy User Sync), job-004 (Legacy Email Campaign Trigger), and job-006 (Legacy Archive Cleanup).
Call delete_job with job_id=job-002 to delete the Legacy User Sync job.
Call delete_job with job_id=job-004 to delete the Legacy Email Campaign Trigger job.
Call delete_job with job_id=job-006 to delete the Legacy Archive Cleanup job.
Call list_jobs (no filter) to retrieve the remaining active jobs for the summary.
Present a summary listing the four remaining jobs — Daily Sales Report, Weekly Inventory Audit, Hourly Health Check, and Monthly Billing Cycle — along with their enabled states and statuses.

check.rubric: The agent should clearly identify all three legacy disabled jobs (job-002 Legacy User Sync, job-004 Legacy Email Campaign Trigger, job-006 Legacy Archive Cleanup), confirm their deletion, and provide a coherent summary of the four remaining active jobs including their names and statuses. The summary should be accurate, complete, and well-organized.

task_name: Delete Inactive Legacy Jobs and Summarize Remaining

prompt: Our infrastructure team has several monitoring jobs that were temporarily paused last quarter. Please re-enable all disabled jobs whose names contain the word "monitor" and change their cron expressions to run every 15 minutes. Report back which jobs were updated and confirm their new schedule.

reference_solution.description: Call list_jobs with a filter for enabled=false (or status=paused) to retrieve all disabled jobs currently in the system.

From the returned list, identify all jobs whose name contains the word "monitor": cpu-monitor-prod (job-001), memory-monitor-staging (job-002), disk-monitor-prod (job-003), and network-monitor-core (job-007). Note that log-cleanup-archive is disabled but does NOT contain "monitor" and must be skipped.

For each of the four identified monitor jobs, call update_job with job_id set to the respective job's ID, enabled set to true, and cron_expression set to "*/15 * * * *". This results in exactly 4 update_job calls.

Compile a summary response listing each updated job by name, confirming they are now enabled, and stating the new cron schedule (every 15 minutes / "*/15 * * * *"). Do not modify or mention daily-backup-db, weekly-report-sales, or log-cleanup-archive.

check.rubric: The agent should clearly list all four monitor jobs that were updated (cpu-monitor-prod, memory-monitor-staging, disk-monitor-prod, network-monitor-core), confirm they were re-enabled, and state their new cron schedule of every 15 minutes (*/15 * * * *). The response should not claim any non-monitor jobs were modified. Score high if the summary is accurate, specific, and well-organized.

task_name: Enable and Reschedule Paused Monitoring Jobs

prompt: Review all scheduled jobs and identify any that run more frequently than every 15 minutes (i.e., cron intervals shorter than 15 minutes). For each such high-frequency job, update its name to include the prefix "HF-" if it doesn't already have it, and add the tag "high-frequency" if it isn't already tagged. Finally, provide a summary of which jobs were modified.

reference_solution.Step 1: Call list_jobs with no filters to retrieve all scheduled jobs.

reference_solution.Step 2: Inspect the cron_expression of each job. Identify jobs running more frequently than every 15 minutes — these are jobs with intervals like */1, */2, */5, or */10 in the minute field.

reference_solution: Step 3: Identify the high-frequency jobs: job-001 (*/5), job-002 (*/10), job-003 (*/2), job-006 (*/1).

reference_solution.Step 4: Check job-003 — it already has the name prefix "HF-" and the tag "high-frequency", so no update is needed.

reference_solution: Step 5: Update job-001 by calling update_job with job_id=job-001 and name='HF-Heartbeat Monitor'.
Step 6: Update job-002 by calling update_job with job_id=job-002 and name='HF-Cache Warmer'.
Step 7: Update job-006 by calling update_job with job_id=job-006 and name='HF-Queue Depth Checker'.

reference_solution.Step 8: Note that tag updates may require re-checking the update endpoint capabilities; if tags can be updated via update_job, include the high-frequency tag for the three modified jobs.

reference_solution.Step 9: Provide a final summary listing job-001, job-002, and job-006 as modified, and job-003 as already compliant. Non-high-frequency jobs (job-004, job-005, job-007) should not be touched.

check.rubric: The agent's final response should include a clear summary listing which jobs were modified (renamed and/or tagged), explain why they were selected (run interval shorter than 15 minutes), and note that job-003 (HF-Metrics Collector) was already compliant and required no changes. The response should be accurate, concise, and reference at least three job names.

prompt: We've accumulated several jobs that perform the same function (database cleanup) but were created multiple times with slightly different names and schedules. Please identify all database cleanup jobs, keep only the one that ran most recently, update its cron expression to run every day at 2:30 AM, and delete all the other duplicate cleanup jobs. Summarize what you did and which job was kept.

reference_solution.description: Call list_jobs to retrieve all scheduled jobs. Review the returned list and identify the five database cleanup jobs by their names and tags: job-001 (DB Cleanup - Primary), job-002 (Database Cleanup Legacy), job-003 (db-cleanup-v2), job-005 (DB Cleanup Temp), and job-007 (DB Cleanup Redundant).
Compare the last_run timestamps of all five cleanup jobs. job-001 has the most recent last_run of 2024-11-20T03:00:00Z, making it the survivor to retain.
Call update_job with job_id=job-001 and cron_expression="30 2 * * *" to reschedule it to run every day at 2:30 AM. Ensure enabled remains true.
Call delete_job four times, once each for job-002, job-003, job-005, and job-007, to remove all duplicate database cleanup jobs.
Do not touch job-004 (nightly-metrics-aggregation) or job-006 (weekly-log-rotation) as they are unrelated jobs.

Summarize the actions taken: identified 5 database cleanup jobs, retained job-001 (DB Cleanup - Primary) as the most recently run, updated its schedule to 30 2 * * *, and deleted the four duplicate jobs (job-002, job-003, job-005, job-007).

check.rubric: The agent should clearly explain which database cleanup jobs were found, why job-001 (DB Cleanup - Primary) was chosen as the survivor (most recent last_run date), confirm that its cron expression was updated to 2:30 AM daily, and list which duplicate job IDs were deleted. The summary should be coherent, accurate, and not mention modifying any non-cleanup jobs.

task_name: Consolidate Duplicate Jobs and Reschedule Survivor

prompt: Our overnight batch processing jobs are all currently scheduled to run at midnight (00:00), which is causing resource contention and timeouts. Please reschedule each of these jobs so they are spread out across the 1:00 AM to 5:00 AM window in 30-minute increments (e.g., 01:00, 01:30, 02:00, 02:30, etc.), assigning slots in the order the jobs were created (by job ID ascending). Make sure all affected jobs remain enabled after the update, and provide a summary of the new schedule.

reference_solution.description: Call list_jobs (optionally filtering by tag "batch" or "overnight") to retrieve all scheduled jobs and identify which ones have cron_expression "0 0 * * *" (midnight). This yields job-001 through job-006. job-007 runs at 06:00 and is excluded.

Sort the identified jobs by job ID ascending: job-001, job-002, job-003, job-004, job-005, job-006. Assign off-peak slots in 30-minute increments starting at 01:00:
  job-001 → "0 1 * * *"  (01:00)
  job-002 → "30 1 * * *" (01:30)
  job-003 → "0 2 * * *"  (02:00)
  job-004 → "30 2 * * *" (02:30)
  job-005 → "0 3 * * *"  (03:00)
  job-006 → "30 3 * * *" (03:30)
For each of the 6 jobs, call update_job with the job's job_id, the new cron_expression, and enabled=true to ensure all jobs are active after the update (including job-004 which was disabled).

Present a summary listing each job name, its old cron expression (0 0 * * *), its new cron expression, and its enabled status. Note that job-007 was intentionally skipped as it was already running at 06:00 and is not an overnight midnight job.

check.rubric: The agent should provide a clear summary table or list showing each of the 6 midnight jobs (job-001 through job-006) mapped to their new cron schedule (01:00, 01:30, 02:00, 02:30, 03:00, 03:30), confirm all jobs are enabled, and note that job-007 was not affected because it was not a midnight job. Score highly if the summary is accurate, complete, and easy to read.

task_name: Rotate Overnight Batch Jobs to Off-Peak Hours

prompt: Several scheduled jobs in our system have been reported as failing due to invalid or incorrect cron expressions. Please review all jobs, identify any with obviously broken or non-standard cron expressions (e.g., fields out of range, wrong number of fields, or expressions that would never fire), fix them with sensible schedules that match the job's apparent purpose based on its name, and leave any correctly configured jobs untouched.

reference_solution.description: Call list_jobs with no filters to retrieve all scheduled jobs in the system.
Inspect each job's cron_expression for validity. A standard cron expression has exactly 5 fields: minute (0-59), hour (0-23), day-of-month (1-31), month (1-12), day-of-week (0-7).

Identify invalid jobs: - job-002: minute=99 (>59) and hour=25 (>23) are both out of range. Fix: "0 8 * * 1" (Monday 8am weekly). - job-003: has 7 fields instead of 5 (looks like seconds/years added). Fix: "0 * * * *" (top of every hour). - job-004: day-of-month=32 is impossible. Fix: "0 9 1 * *" (1st of each month at 9am). - job-008: day-of-week=8 is invalid (valid range is 0-7, where 7=Sunday). Fix: "0 3 * * 5" (every Friday at 3am for bi-weekly approximation or use "0 3 1,15 * *").
Call update_job for each of the four invalid jobs (job-002, job-003, job-004, job-008) with corrected cron expressions.
Leave jobs job-001, job-005, job-006, and job-007 untouched as they have valid cron expressions.
Report back to the user summarizing which jobs were broken, what was wrong with each expression, and what replacement expressions were applied.

check.rubric: The agent should correctly identify exactly four jobs with invalid cron expressions: job-002 (minute 99, hour 25 are out of range), job-003 (7 fields instead of 5), job-004 (day-of-month 32 is invalid), and job-008 (day-of-week 8 is invalid, valid range is 0-7). Valid jobs (001, 005, 006, 007) must NOT be modified. The replacement cron expressions should be sensible and match the job's apparent purpose (e.g., weekly-report on a valid weekday, hourly-health-check with 5 fields, monthly-invoice on day 1, bi-weekly-security-scan on a valid day-of-week). Score highly if all four broken jobs are fixed with reasonable replacements and no valid jobs are touched.

task_name: Identify and Fix Misconfigured Jobs with Invalid Cron Expressions
